title: Flavorful Turkish Pasta Pilaf
description: A jammy, chewy, lazy-sunday Turkish dish featuring Parmigiano-Reggiano, pasta-water-emulsion, and low-fat-friendly ingredients, cooked to perfection in a kazan cauldron.

ingredients.name: pasta
ingredients.notes: short, sturdy shapes like penne or rigatoni

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large kazan cauldron over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes, until it is fragrant and darkened slightly.
Add the pasta, chicken broth, salt, black pepper, and paprika to the cauldron.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, until the pasta is al dente.
Stir in the grated Parmigiano-Reggiano until it is well combined and the sauce is creamy.
Remove the cauldron from the heat and let it cool slightly, allowing the flavors to meld together.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with additional Parmigiano-Reggiano if desired.
